summary: Rule chunk from Gamemastering Paranoia page 92, chunk 2.

mechanics.raw_text:
Paranoia sessions are ideally brief, intense, and fast-paced, and starting off a ses sion by rolling up characters is like stating a James Bond movie with twenty minutes of ‘Meet the Press’éstyle talking-heads exposition. For more experienced and ambitious players, cooperative character design is fun, Give your players a rough outline of their characters and, let them fil in the details. In any case, you'll still have to work up the distinctively paranoid refinements every character should have: * Some possession that is ambiguously valuable, partially incomprehensible, and eminently treasonous. ' Current secret society status — reputation within the group, standing orders, special in- teress, etc ‘Tidbits about other characters — vague rumors, fragmentary evidence, puzzling obser- vations — that foment distrust and provide tan- 
talizing hearsay evidence of treason, (Note: This is not always necessary — sometimes throwing Complete strangers together works just fine.) 
Gaps ad Seating Order Get in the habit of going around the table from left to right when players declare their actions, Get a quick statement from the frst, character, go immediately to the person next to him, and s0 on — bang, bang, bang, No stalling, no going back for second thoughts, ‘no interrupting with out-of-order comments.
